The tornado destroyed several RV units and caused extensive damage to the resort's infrastructure, including the loss of roofs, snapped trees, and the displacement of heavy objects. The extent of the damage indicated that the tornado was powerful and caused considerable destruction to the property.

results.question: When did the Storm Prediction Center begin monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event?
results.student_answer: The Storm Prediction Center began monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event on May 3, 2020, at 7:24 AM CDT. They issued a moderate risk for tornadoes in parts of Louisiana and Mississippi, highlighting the potential for strong to violent tornadoes. This warning was part of their efforts to alert the public and emergency management officials to the significant tornado threat that was expected to develop throughout the day.

results.question: When did the Kentwood–Carson tornado occur and what were its start and end times?
results.reference_answer: Saturday, March 15, 2025; it began at 12:17 p.m. CDT and dissipated at 1:39 p.m. CDT.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado occurred on May 22, 2020. The start time was 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00), and the end time was approximately 3:45 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00).

results.question: Where did the Kentwood–Carson tornado begin?
results.reference_answer: One mile east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado began in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.

results.question: How many people did the EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado kill?
results.student_answer: The EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado did not result in any fatalities.

results.question: What maximum wind speed and Enhanced Fujita rating did the National Weather Service assign to the tornado?
results.reference_answer: Maximum winds of 170 mph (270 km/h), rated a low-end EF4.
results.student_answer: The National Weather Service assigned a maximum wind speed of 170 mph (270 km/h) and an Enhanced Fujita (EF) rating of EF3 to the tornado.

results.question: What damage did the tornado cause to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County?
results.reference_answer: It destroyed all 27 cabins and 210 campsites.
results.student_answer: The tornado caused significant damage to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County, including the destruction of multiple RVs and the collapse of a large portion of the roof of the main lodge.
